About the Author

Nora Ashworth

Nora Ashworth writes emotionally rich fiction that explores the complexities of human relationships, the weight of secrets, and the identities we construct for ourselves. Her work delves into the past that refuses to let go — examining how love, loss, and self-discovery shape who we become.

With a background in psychology and literature, Nora brings psychological depth to her characters, creating narratives that resonate long after the final page. Her stories are for readers who seek meaning in the ordinary moments and truth in the spaces between words.

Follow on TikTok

— Nora

Featured Release

The Weight of Her Silence

★★★★★

When Nora Calloway drives four hours south from Atlanta to help her seventy-eight-year-old aunt sort through a locked back room, she expects dust and old furniture. She does not expect a cedar box — or a name that rewrites everything her mother ever believed about who she is.

A quiet, emotionally precise novel about the secrets families keep, the cost of protection that becomes erasure, and what it means to finally say the true thing — even when it's too late to fix everything, and just in time to fix something.

Available on Amazon Kindle

$4.99 Read Now →
All Books

The Collection

Seven novels. Seven emotional journeys. All available on Kindle.

View full author page on Amazon →
Buy on Amazon

The Woman Who Understood Others

Istanbul, November. Vera Solano has built a careful life — until a DNA test reveals she isn't who she says she is. A novel about identity, survival, and what it means to finally tell the truth to the people you love most.

$4.99 · Kindle →
Buy on Amazon

The Weight of Her Silence

A locked back room. A cedar box. A name no one ever said out loud. Three generations of women, one inherited silence, and the cost of protection that became erasure.

$4.99 · Kindle →
Buy on Amazon

The Last Trip

A daughter, her aging father, and a road trip to the town he left forty years ago — and never explained why. A quiet, devastating novel about family secrets and the courage to knock on a door you should have knocked on decades ago.

$4.99 · Kindle →
Buy on Amazon

Only in German

A cardboard box. A sealed envelope addressed to a name she's never heard. A novel about what silence becomes when it can't be said — and what it means to be truly present before it's too late.

$4.99 · Kindle →
Buy on Amazon

Everything the Body Knows

Three years after their relationship ended, she calls him with a diagnosis. They are not together. They are simply there. A story about love that survives its own ending, and presence as its own kind of devotion.

$2.99 · Kindle →
Buy on Amazon

The Draft

Vera returns to her grandmother's house in Olinda, Brazil, as executor — and finds a draft never sent, from a year never spoken of. A quiet novel about inheritance, silence, and the letter you've carried for sixty years.

$4.99 · Kindle →
Buy on Amazon

The Language I Forgot

Carrying her mother's ashes across the Aegean, Elena learns that grief isn't just losing a person — it's losing everything they chose not to tell you. A novel about inheritance, and what we must travel far to find.

$4.99 · Kindle →
"

The past is never truly past. It lives in us, shapes us, and sometimes, it saves us.

— From The Weight of Her Silence
